mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: RSAFS year-over-year growth in the top quartile fails to yield positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of signals over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) drops below 5.0%, it signals a tightening labor market that will eventually lead to wage inflation and Fed tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE below 5.0% is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or Rank IC for UNRATE level is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A negative real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) stimulates equity markets by reducing the discount rate on future earnings. The interaction of low nominal rates and rising inflation creates an especially bullish environment.
failure_condition: The spread between CPI inflation and Fed funds rate fails to have a statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns (p > 0.10) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity. In a late-cycle expansion regime, such a contraction is a leading indicator of a recession, depressing equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines lead to positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases during late-cycle expansion periods, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 10% month-over-month indicates a weakening housing market, which historically precedes economic downturns and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
failure_condition: Following a 10% monthly drop in HOUST, 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 40% of instances, or the signal yields a negative average return.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The monthly change in PAYEMS in the top decile produces negative 5-day returns in more than 40% of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Combining high GDP growth (GDPC1) with declining unemployment (UNRATE) provides a robust signal of economic strength that sustains equity gains over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth > 2.0% annualized and UNRATE declining over the same quarter
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term is not statistically significant in a regression model (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, robust payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals overheating that will prompt aggressive Fed tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 2.5% and PAYEMS monthly change > 200,000
failure_condition: During high CPI regimes, strong payrolls lead to positive 20-day returns in more than 35% of cases, or the signal does not achieve a negative average return over walk-forward testing.
